description:
You are a build-and-release engineer who needs a quick, ad-hoc way to expose the day’s build artifacts through a lightweight web server so that QA can pick them up.

Goal:
1. Create a new directory named /home/user/artifacts
2. Inside that directory, create an HTML landing page named index.html that simply displays the heading “Build Artifacts Repository” (exact text, without quotes, inside an <h1> element).
3. Launch a minimal web server that:
   • Listens on TCP port 8123  
   • Serves files rooted at /home/user/artifacts  
   • Runs in the background so the terminal prompt is returned
4. Immediately after launching the server, append a two-line log entry to /home/user/webserver_launch.log using the exact format shown below (replace the bracketed placeholders with real values at run time):
   Line 1: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS STARTED on PORT 8123 serving /home/user/artifacts  
   Line 2: PID: <process_id_of_the_running_web_server>

   • The timestamp must be in 24-hour ISO-like format shown above.  
   • There should be exactly one space between each token.  
   • Do not add extra lines before or after these two lines.

Success criteria that the automated grader will check:

A. Directory /home/user/artifacts exists.  
B. File /home/user/artifacts/index.html exists and, when read, contains exactly one line:  
   <html><body><h1>Build Artifacts Repository</h1></body></html>  
   (case-sensitive, no trailing spaces).  
C. A process is listening on port 8123 and its command line shows it is serving /home/user/artifacts.  
D. File /home/user/webserver_launch.log exists and its last two lines follow the format described in step 4.

Use any standard tool available on a typical Linux install (e.g. Python’s built-in http.server) and keep the solution to roughly 3–4 shell commands. You do not need root privileges. No additional packages should be installed. Make sure the server remains running after your commands complete so that the grader can connect to it.
truth:
Directory /home/user/artifacts must exist. File /home/user/artifacts/index.html must contain exactly:

Build Artifacts Repository

A process should be running whose command line includes “http.server 8123” (or equivalent) and “--directory /home/user/artifacts” (if using Python ≥3.7) or is started from that directory; it must be listening on TCP port 8123. The last two lines of /home/user/webserver_launch.log are the only ones that will be graded. Their regex patterns: Line 1: ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} STARTED on PORT 8123 serving /home/user/artifacts$ Line 2: ^PID:\s+[0-9]+$ The PID number must correspond to the process actually listening on port 8123 at grading time.
